top_categories.workout_context: 62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Pedal IMUA's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
94ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Pedal IMUA's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
At Pedal IMUA, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Base fitness for Pedal IMUA. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Pedal IMUA. Train it until it feels boring.
5,800ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
5,800ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 62 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Low-cadence force work for 5,800ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Pedal IMUA, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: December in Maui, Hawaii, USA. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Pedal IMUA — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Pedal IMUA’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Pedal IMUA requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Maui, Hawaii, USA.
